title: Delissea rockii
taxonomy: Delissea rockii is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is a type of bellflower native to Hawaii. Delissea rockii is most closely related to other plants in the Campanulaceae family, such as Lobelia and Canarina. The plant's classification is further broken down into the genus Delissea and the species D. rockii. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and genetic makeup.

intro: Delissea rockii, also known as the Hawaiian hound's tongue, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the island of Kauai in Hawaii. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in soothing wounds, reducing fever, and treating various infections.
